channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: BEARISH ON COSTCO — 100 replies — 400.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
BEARISH ON COSTCO

Open thread • Replies: 100 • Updated: 2026-07-07 17:57 UTC

OP

People are scamming costcos lenient return policy now. Should we short COST?

Video:
https://x.com/Basedgoymemes/status/2073860759269077199

Early replies (first 3)

62449396
based

62449400
I wouldn't call jeets 'people'

62449441
Fuck this racist, post the person who started to do this was some white bitch, she did it for 12 years

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62454398
>>62454145
Kek

62454476
just wait, in 2 more years costco will have a jeet ceo and instead of hotdogs youre gonna get currywurst

62454493
>>62454349
The richest cohort in America shopping there and white incels are shorting? Kektop!

62454541
>>62454493
Seethe

62454717
>>62451853
>Turns every one of those companies into scamming pieces of shit
What exactly are you proud of, you stupid shit eating jeet?

62455561
>>62454717
Kek

62455654
>>62454717
Beating the white incel losers at the game of life and hearing their cries bbbbbbut im white

62455686
>>62453347
>us
Your skin is the same color as poo

62455702
>>62455654
>t.

62455754
>>62454135

62455759
>>62455686
You cant breed, kektop

62455766
>>62453370

62455771
>>62455759
Post stack
But you won't because you're poor and you'll always be poor.

62455822
>>62455771
Rocks? Lmao what a loser

62455829
>>62455822
Sour grapes

62455840
>>62455822
You can't even afford rocks? How poor are you? Answer: extremely poor.

62455845
>>62453347
Indian immigrants are the richest because they all want to escape their country. Most other countries are livable and civilized, so rich people from other countries like to stay in their own country.

Nothing to be proud of.

62455854
>>62455845
Your country is a shithole and you hate it

62455855
>>62455759
Indian men are at the absolute bottom of the sexual pecking order in Western nations.

Projection.

62455859
>>62455854
No country is a worse shithole than India Pakistan Bangladesh. Not sure what you meant by that

62455884
>>62449441
t. brown piece of garbage kek

62455902
>>62449769
You're an invading piece of shit.
I make a point of openly yelling at one brown retard every day at least; you fuckers were born to be yelled at.
Fucking retards who can't follow the most basic rules of civilization, third world trash

62455914
>>62455859
You white incel losers hate your own country and constantly wish it is destroyed. Totally bizarre behavior

62455930
>>62455902
Cry more incel, 1000 of my relatives invade every day. Its our country niw

62455936
>>62449441
Please leave anytime.

62455942
>>62455914
>incel losers
You're an expert on the subject from your daily first hand experience

62456019
>>62455942
I would like to thank the white incel losers for letting us have your jobs, your women and your country

62456057
>>62449441

>self reporting JEET gets caught online defending his street shitting brother

Imagine the smell of that chair

62456250
>>62456057
Kek

62456293
>>62455930
Kek, we stopped letting you dirty fuckers in last year, we have enough people serving coffee and doing menial jobs already.
You faggots are all 5'6" and stink like either vile BO or a whole bottle of nigger-scented baby powder wash.
Being brown and ugly is a curse from god himself; you are marked brown like shit and can never wash it off. Ugly jeet kek

item.title: Any active duty US military members or veterans on /biz/? — 90 replies — 360.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Any active duty US military members or veterans on /biz/?

Open thread • Replies: 90 • Updated: 2026-07-07 18:04 UTC

OP

Any active duty US military members or veterans on /biz/?

I joined the military and I wanna know how to get a million dollars. I put 10% of my income into my retirement account and the rest of my free cash into index funds.

Early replies (first 3)

62447635
>>62447595
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sZZaOR4UCnE

62447641
>>62447595
The only smart play is to aim for a 100% disability rating which you can get in ~1 to 4 years (1 enlistment).
I know several guys getting $4000+ a month for life.
I am a vet and I get nothing.

62447664
>>62447595
If you're not high rank or close to information in some other way then it's just another job. Not gonna change much in terms of how getting a million would look like, you'll have to gamble like the rest of us.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62455029
>>62451309
How am I fucking way the fuck up by not taking a 6% interest mortgage on a house with 2% property taxes that appreciates significantly slower than the stock market, when I can just leave the money in index funds and watch it grow at a much higher rate?

The couple hundred dollars you pocket a month over your mortgage, HOA, fees and repairs is not going to beat leaving the principle in the stock market to compound. I'll buy a house when I'm rich.

62455051
>>62451419
Was never in but from a military family. Things went to shit after 2000. Family is too in the know on how everyone is getting screwed by the VA office. Especially their friends who had kids join. Only one dumb cousin who didn't get his benefits package. Same thing happened with most of his buddies. Exemplary service. Oops briefly questioned an order in boot camp on day five. No $35,000 package for you. He would have been better off if he shot himself in the foot a month later. No joke.

62455132
>>62447595
I'm working on getting disability now bros but I don't see it happening with the evidence I got. Looking for mental stuff. I got
>2 C&P exams with “At least as likely as not" for Depression and anxiety
>Independent medical nexus opinion for the same
>Self harm scars from time in service
>Buddy statement from someone who saw them while I was in
>Statement from someone who saw them right after I got out
I never went to behavioral health so that might just be an automatic"not service connected". I read online of people getting connected with just Buddy statements but I figure I don't have a very good chance. If I get denied and then my appeal gets denied should I get a lawyer or go through a service or something?

62455158
>>62455132
wtf where is this gif from?

62455177
>>62447595
>>62448022
What's your branch and MOS? Make sure you get everything that hurts documented. Even if you sprained your ankle and would be good with just Advil or something get it documented by the clinic. Also get a security clearance if you have the opportunity. I make $150k with it alone

62455186
>>62455158
Planet earth or another high budget nature documentary. They'd be the only ones capable of getting this magnificent(and elusive) creature on film

62455219
>>62455051
What the fuck are you talking about man, theres no such thing as a "benefits package". What $35,000 after finishing boot camp?

Just because your dad and uncles scammed disability by claiming they hurt their back after one enlistment in the 80s doesn't mean they're experts on VA disability. You sound like a retard and should do some bare-minimum googling before you speak on this subject.

62455354
>>62455013
>>62455051
You are an agitator shill. I doubt you are going on 45+ years old like you claim, since you are too retarded to live.
There is no such thing as a VA "$35,000 package".

VA is the VETERANS AFFAIRS OFFICE. You have to be in reserves with service related conditions or out of active duty.
The VA office is Workmans comp, DO YOU UNDERSTAND?
You have to actually BE INJURED or HAVE A LIFE LONG LASTING CONDITION spurred on DURING SERVICE to claim benefits.

I'm capitalizing this shit because you're too stupid to know it.
>Oops briefly questioned an order in boot camp on day five.
You can't get your discharge status changed by the VA.
What you're suggesting is that an honorable discharge can be changed by the VA to a dishonorable.
Bullshit.
Your family is lying to you and you're gullible or you are legitimately shilling in bad faith. Probably the latter given how low effort this is.

I bet I'm talking to pic rel.

62455470
>>62455354
How does one get your job? No trolling, i would actually do it.

62455496
>>62455470
are you implying he's a paid shill because he's explaining the extremely basic, google'able premise of VA disability?

62455518
>>62455496
How do i get your job, then? And what is the pay?

62455520
>>62455496
Im waiting, i dont have all day.

62455528
>>62455470
>>62455518
Juet give up, Robert. I can spot you on any board, in any thread.
Your tells are too obvious like complaining about /k/ in your first post.
And now, giving rapid short replies when caught.
It's over.

62455545
>>62455518
>>62455520
>>62455520
Sign up right here

https://gprivate.com/6ligz

62455549
>>62455520
>>62455518
>>62455470
Data center Technician ($150k). Nta

62455572
>>62455528
>>Calls me robert.
I see, it appears your technical skills are not good enough to find out my real name in short time. Does that mean the government outsources propaganda to jeets, or are they really just that bad at eglin air base?

62455580
>>62455177
Air Force 12X

62455670
>>62455572
I know Robert isn't your real name. It's the pet name that was given to you for being such a terrorist worshiping ingrate.
The name you actually like to post as is Gort Torkenson which is also the name of your twitter.
Your entire life's mission is to lie about the military.
You doordash for a living (as you posted on /k/) and spend all your money gambling in casinos on Sunday (as you posted on your twitter).

62455722
>>62455670
Fucking hilarious

62455733
>>62455670
Looks like i will be gort torkenson from now on

62455735
>>62455670
Thanks man you made my day you have no idea how funny that is

62455851
>>62455132
you need to mention tinnitus as well. And other stuff that can't actually be measured or confirmed

62455876
>>62455851
They patched the tinnitus method

62455879
>>62455851
Sinusitis is a good one too if you're going that route. Didn't go into it because OP said he wasn't going the scamming VA route

62455958
Mandatory Robert alert. /biz/ has no fucking idea how big of a lolcow this faggot is on /k/ and it is hilarious that he has been bullied so hard that he has now resorted to using other boards and linking posts to /k/ just so he can talk shit about the military that kicked him out after he openly proclaimed he would murder women and children if he deployed in Afghanistan.

62456014
>>62455958
White incel loser seethe

62456157
>>62447595
Classic wow is so peak

62456297
>>62447595
>Any active duty US military members or veterans

Israeli citizen here. Even though it's your duty, thank you for your service.

Sidenote: would you mind prepaying your '26 US taxes? The wife and I are looking to re-do the kitchen. Thanks

62456299
>>62456297
This one is Robert also.

62456310
>>62455958
/k/ bans you for asking questions that they don't have pre-scripted answers for or you just ask about how to reload a certain gun safely and what bullets it uses. Forgetting there are also hunters and self defense people who would be interested in that board. Then they ban you for every post you made that didn't break their arbitrary rules days apart. Can't crack on topic jokes there or anything. It is all about conditioning people to join the military with group think. Hunters can't do lets see your rack jokes or talk about the best guns and knives for civilian use at all. Sucks I have to go elsewhere on the internet. Once in a while we get someone who got out of the military and escaped the /k/ hive mind. OP isn't it. He is an annoying recruiter who get ridiculed after he leaves his /k/ containment board.

item.title: House buying — 86 replies — 344.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
House buying

Open thread • Replies: 86 • Updated: 2026-07-07 18:02 UTC

OP

I have $180k sitting in an S&P500 index fund and every normie I meet says I'm making a mistake by not getting a mortgage ASAP because housing will only get more expensive.

What is the /biz/ philosophy on this advice? I'm a rentoid and currently pay only around $1000 a month.

Early replies (first 3)

62447251
>>62447246
>every normie I meet says
You could buy and then the market severely crashes.
Housing shouldn't be a financial asset but that's corporatism for you.

62447257
>>62447251
Yea i guess but the stock market "could" also crash and i lose everything. That's not really insightful advice.

62447265
Lmao you are a rentoid

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62448596
>>62447246
>because housing will only get more expensive.
Maybe, likely so for big cities that get more job offerings and infinite jeets/immigrants. Rural areas? I am not sure, those might start to drop once boomers die off and zoomers/alphas are too poor and low in numbers and not interested in rural houses.

62448620
>>62448596
Incel rentoids now experts on the housing market, is there anything they dont know?

62448624
>>62448620
I am a proud owner of a commieblock apartment and some fields and forestland that I got from my parents/grandparents.

62448648
>>62448441
Build from scratch be your own contractor

62448659
>>62448624
Thats nice maybe keep out of discussions on America if from commieblock land

62448682
>>62448659
>implying you're in the US
idiot. If we kick the foreigners out you'll be gone first.

62448689
Normies are not getting mortgages because rates are like 6.5% when they would not normally be 3%>.

62448696
>>62448689
Boomers paid 20%

62448701
>>62447286
>2nd house for 425k around 31.
>2nd house is worth 900k now
What happened is their dollars lost half the purchasing power.
BTW they didn't but the house for $425k. They bought it for that plus whatever they pay in interest over time. Which is very likely to be closer to the amount they sold it for later.
It's nice to build equity, but don't be a fool.

62449002
>>62447246
>I have $180k sitting in an S&P500 index fund and every normie I meet says I'm making a mistake by not getting a mortgage ASAP because housing will only get more expensive.
>What is the /biz/ philosophy on this advice? I'm a rentoid and currently pay only around $1000 a month.
I'm in a similar position to you and unless you have a lifestyle reason to need a house it doesn't make financial sense right now IMO. Look at it financially, houses cost probably on the ballpark of 300-350k, let's just assume 325k median in a low to medium cost area, that's a 145k loan, at 6.25% interest rate the annual loss of interest only of 9k on year 1 and 8.9k on year 2 contrasted with an annual net loss currently of 12k a year renting but you also dont have other costs like property taxes, insurance, and maintenance costs that should be baked into rent but are not going to baked into that interest loss of yhe house so add those in too, where i live I'd pay 6k prop tax, 1k insurance, and rule of thumb is to budget 1-3% of home value so 3.25k-9.8k. To build on the case if you have money in the SP500 it has returned 10.5% annually (18.9k) or if you want to play it safe 4.5% in CDs (8.1k). That said, it depends on how long you are looking to buy a house, how risk tolerant you are, and if your landlord wants to increase rents. Run those numbers down a spreadsheet and see what makes sense for you in your area but i have come to the conclusion of letting my money ride in the market for the time being knowing the risk that I could get burned.

62450291
>>62447246
my advice is quit snooping around your mom's secret book collection

62450516
average home price prior to covid was 4.5 years of salary; now its closer to 6.8 years of salary. prices are too high and is way cheaper to rent for now. i save while renting to buy outright, fuck a mortgage.

62450518
>>62448701
>nice to build equity
>depreciating asset requires upkeep
>no where to go but up

ive seen this movie before, sometime around 2008 i think

62450563
>>62450516
Thats a you problem if you cant double your wage post covid

62450591
I know this is biz but everyone here is talking about buying a house as though you have to make money off the decision, I could care less about making money off my house, I bought it because it was a huge lifestyle upgrade and now I live 100x better than when I was renting an apartment, it feels like I finally started my real life.

62450594
>>62447246
Let the boomers deal in the real world. Housing is a terrible investment if there is any risk to your income over the term of the loan. My cousin sold TSLA to put a down-payment on a home $120k. The most money he ever had. Home price was $230k. Turned out the home was a lemon and he had dumped another $80k into repairs. Guess what? He lost the house when he and his wife's winery side hustle failed. $200k given to bank for a couple years being a homeowner. Had he put the money into an index fund he'd be worth half a million by now.

62450595
houses are for living in, not speculation

62450600
>>62450594
the math on this story isn't adding up, bro had a $110K loan after that down payment which is only like $500 a month, sounds like he was just bad at finance if he lost it with that payment

62450606
>>62448058
Jealous blk boi

62450608
>>62450600
Rentoids all suck at math their anecdotes are fake as fuck but real in their bitter minds

62450897
My house is paid off. Feels great man. 6 bedrooms 3.5 baths on half an acre. Great home for my family of 6.

62451180
>>62450600
You can't even follow basic math. He had a $190k loan at around 6%. Hardly $500 a month with insurance, taxes, mortgage insurance, etc.

>>62450608
My cost to rent is 11% of my take home pay. You are spending more on a mortgage with no guarantee of keeping your home.

Biz really has the dumbest anons.

62454567
>>62447246
I unironically read this book

62454883
>>62450897
Sounds shitty man, i can just save and invest without being bled dry by a bunch of kids who are gonna grow up trans

62455019
>>62447246
When you buy a house, if you do, if you're going to rent it out, make sure you put a large enough down payment to make it cash flow $600+/mo.
Basically getting your money back little by little
and that 600 surplus will cover expenses as they come up, and someone else pays the equity for you, and you get the interest & depreciation deductions.
One big mistake with my rentals is putting the minimum down.
Expenses come out of my pocket mostly.
Remember taxes go up, insurance goes up, rezoned to a FEMA flood zone to lay for coastal homes, HOA goes up, everything goes up, so even if you increase rent, you're not taking it home, because you're paying those increased costs which go up every year.
You can make money in RE if you can fix the places yourself. If you're not handy? Think about something else.
>t. not handy and it costs me an arm and a leg and construction contractors are the worst, even the "premium" ones can't install a level floor.

62456167
>>62447246
Houses are for gays. Real chads life onnastreets with a dividend portfolio.

62456179
When the cashier at 7-11 gives housing advice the market is going to fall out.

62456187
>>62448648
>That'll be 50k to hook up your house to utilities and its mandatory in this county otherwise we condemn your building.

62456204
180k isn't enough
you need to make 100k a year to enjoy owning a home having a family etc

62456307
>>62447246
Rentoids will spend 30 years paying off someone else's house just to tell you you're bad at investing.
Bull owns the land.
Cuck pays the bull every month.
If you don't own land by retirement, you got outplayed.

item.title: Why do people act like buying a house is a good investment >Property taxes >Maintenance >Repairs because something WILL… — 37 replies — 148.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why do people act like buying a house is a good investment >Property taxes >Maintenance >Repairs because something WILL…

Open thread • Replies: 37 • Updated: 2026-07-07 17:58 UTC

OP

Why do people act like buying a house is a good investment
>Property taxes
>Maintenance
>Repairs because something WILL go wrong
>Even if you're "beating inflation" with a mortgage you're paying much more than its actual cost
>Closing costs
>All just so you can sell it (likely at a loss when considering inflation) so you can buy a slightly less expensive house
It's not that buying a house is a bad purchase if you can afford it, but acting like it's somehow an "investment" is absolutely retarded, you'd probably make more back from buying Pokemon cards

Early replies (first 3)

62450381
>>62450377
You're supposed to buy houses on a discount
>t. Selling for double what I purchased in 2023 after $20K in upgrades LOL

62450391
>>62450377
Idk I just want one to live in, but maybe they rent it that way person who rents pays bills and when he break something you can tell him to pay for it and then when they don't give a fuck anymore they sell it

62450397
>cbf moving every year because the landlord decides they a wankers and want to sell to some Chinese immigrants who took our jobs

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62450446
>>62450377
It’s hard to lose money from owning a house. What are the alternatives? Either rent where you get no money back, or if you’re lucky enough to live with parents who don’t ask for anything. Tax is a non issue. One year of tax is the equivalent of a renter paying one months rent. Maintenance is virtually cost free. Cleaning up the house is what any responsible human being does regardless of whether they own a house or not. Repairs happen anywhere. Learn to DIY. You shouldn’t need to spend $400 for someone to replace a $40 faucet that takes 30 minutes to do. It’s not only about investing from a financial point of view either. Owning a home is more piece of mind and being able to do whatever you want (not counting HOAs). That alone is worth it more than anything else

62450464
Renting when old will be a massive issue in years to come.
Pensions will not cover rent AND living for the vast majority of people.
It's an issue not reared its head yet because boomers own homes and have pensions that cover their living, or they are selling their homes for care.
Unless you know you are going to be wealthy, you need to own a home at some point or be utterly doomed to hardship in an already strained system in your old age.

>inb4 i'll just rope when old LULZ
Childish nonsense talk.

62451291
>>62450377
>Property taxes
>Maintenance
>Repairs because something WILL go wrong
all of this is wrapped up in your rent payment dumbass. you really think those costs don’t exist for your landlord and he’s not passing them on to you, plus profit?

62451450
buying a house cured my depression
best investment ever

62452006
when the market crashes everyone will still own their homes and will have enjoyed living in them but you'll own nothing and will have been living in an apartment listening to blacks keep you awake all night all that time

62452049
>>62450401
yea this tracks. it's like how a 401k is "good" in that it keeps retard normies from spending that chunk of their paycheck on coke and sportsball tickets

a good rule of thumb

62453524
>>62450377
I bought a 3-bedroom and rent out two of the bedrooms. My tenants pay my mortgage for me.
Housing was an "investment" for the last half-century or so, which is a big part of why things are so fucked now in the housing market.

Also, you're already paying for all that shit when you pay rent. You can either pay your rent to a bank directly or pay it to some retard who is skimming off the top. This realization is what led me to buy a house eventually.

62453548
An asset that can bring rent and only goes up in price. There is literally NO comparable investment. The only people crying about it are poor renters.

62453561
>>62450377
I don't like landlords telling me what I can and can't do with their property. I don't like having neighbors upstairs or downstairs. I don't like having to ask if I want something remodeled or changed. I don't like fighting with my neighbors for parking or who gets to use the yard or the laundry room.

also my house costs 1/5 what rent does because I've had it for 25 years and my payment never goes up while your rent has gone up 600% in the same time period.

62453579
>>62450377
You have to pay all that if you rent, except repair for damage that you didn't cause.

62454390
>>62450377
better than being a rentoid though

62454582
>>62454390
if you pay property taxes, you are a rentoid

62454589
>>62454582
>renter pays $48k to live in a house where I am
>I pay $4k in property taxes
somehow I don't think my $333 per month in "rent" is equal to your $4000 per month in rent

62455984
It WAS a good investment when I bought one in 2020 at $250k with 2.1% APR

62455992
>>62454582
The goys actually think they own their property such delusion like >>62454589

62456012
>>62455992
White incel loser seethe

62456017
>>62450377
That's the fundamental issue with the housing market, dumb ass boomers looking at their home as their investment portfolio. Housing has never been anything more than a stable asset, and that's due to land, appreciating with inflation, despite the depreciation of the home. These dumb ass boomers got hoodwinked, and instead of realizing and admitting they squandered their insanely good fortune by building and buying insanely over priced McMansions. They hold on to this retarded idea that the poorly built shit box is worth a million bucks. One day they may realize, but until they do, we're all fucked.

62456023
>>62456017
>Waaaaaaaaah im a loser cuz of old people not giving me their house

62456036
>>62452006
Last time the market crashed millions of homeowners went underwater.

62456053
>>62450446
It's very easy to lose money owning a house, if you buy a house in an area that then suffers from ghettofication

62456063
Rentoids will spend 30 years paying off someone else's house just to tell you you're bad at investing.
Bull owns the land.
Cuck pays the bull every month.
If you don't own land by retirement, you got outplayed.

62456064
yeah horrible investment
rent is due on the 1st wagie btw the price is going up 5% due to ongoing maintenance issues. please understand have a nice day

62456116
>>62450377
Housing is usually a good investment, because, in many countries, the line is artificially made to go up over time, such as by constraining supply. The housing market is deliberately designed as a giant wealth transfer scheme from younger generation to the older generation, because old people have high turnout on election day, and young people don't. In a well-functioning housing market, the value of a house would slowly fall over time, in line with physical depreciation of the house.

62456159
>>62450377
I agree. It's land that is the real asset. Bought with cash; you own nothing if you have a mortgage.

62456166
>>62456116
This. Property is more of a commodity. The price stays high because of artificial supply constraints.

62456177
>>62450381
bragging that he can't afford to move

62456181
>>62450446
yeah but I can rent for half the price of a mortgage and then the other half gets 10% returns tax free and also I get to move when I want to because I don't have a family

62456269
>>62450377
It's for really rich people who need diversified sources of investments but aren't so rich they can go to a hedge fund. Plus you can actually live there, and you can't live in a hedge fund.

62456282
>>62456036
being under water is only a problem if you want to sell or you can’t pay your mortgage.
you bought your house to live in it, right? you can still live in a house if you owe more than it’s worth.

62456296
>>62456282
The oppurtunity cost fucks you if your 600k house goes down to 450k, the average normie would rope.

item.title: UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #2 — 17 replies — 68.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #2

Open thread • Replies: 17 • Updated: 2026-07-07 17:59 UTC

OP

Brothers,

We spent 20 days and 203 posts together in the last thread and we have yet to formulate a method that will enable us to sit at the table in the six figure club with the rest of kinsmen. We knew this wouldn’t be easy so let's not get dispassionate.

For my next proposal I will say we focus on developing a skill that will increase our value. If we all level up on our own it will only take longer. If we all level up by 2-3 points a month then cumulatively we will all have gotten stronger as a mass society. Perhaps one amongst us can elevate themselves and come back to teach the others!

How long shall we be waiting then? How long will our own potential be mocked by others? To what limit will we continue to persist in the underclass?

Come, those who have not $10,000 to their name

Let us commence the general!

>Good /biz/ manga
https://w9.kaijimanga.com/

>Good /biz/ movies
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Born_Rich_(2003_film)

>Good /biz/ books
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Gambler_(novel)

>Good /biz/ games
https://mahjongsoul.yo-star.com/

>Good /biz/ waifus
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Elizabeth_Holmes

>Agenda 2030 is on the way, have you studied what your enemy has planned out?
https://www.math.uwaterloo.ca/~ervrscay/TheGreatReset.pdf

Praeviously
>>62373752

Early replies (first 3)

62453729
All you faggots need to do is buy chainlink. Fuck your side hustles. You can get a suicide stack for 8k right now.

62453795
Last thread we spoke a lot on learning new skills but very few fruitful things arose. I realized the issue is that a lot of the proposed skills were just boring or too niche.

62453799
>>62453795
wait, are you asking poor people to discuss amongst themselves how to get rich?
kek

you don't learn how to get rich from poor people

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62453805
>>62453622

>you want six figures? Power wash 100 driveways for $100 each, there’s your six figures

62453846
>>62453799
Which came first, the poor or the rich?

62453864
>>62453846
Adam was rich. Then Eve the poor floozy showed up.

62454032
>>62453864
Blasphemy

62454046
>>62453622
If you weren't part of the top 10% in 2025, you're lower class

62454048
>>62453846
first there were only the strong and the weak, with the invention of property this evolved into the rich and poor.

62454058
i missed the last thread. anyway, what worked for me was a temp agency. worked for six months then walked away. back to NEETdom. i think it's best to learn new skills if you can find someone to pay you while you learn.

62454093
Best thread on /biz/ fuck the bezosposters and the ocpdfags

62454116
Some helpful posts from the last thread

62454170
I made 10k in the last 2 months from my waging

62454624
Bump

62455030
>>62454170
Overtime?

62456298
Bump

THIS IS GENTLEMAN SAAAARS! BITCOIN 1000K EOY!

62456120
>>62456114

62456137

58k --> 64k

oh baggieeeeee

62456145
>>62456120
>>62456137
Kek my niggers, you make me bullish a little now.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62456183
>>62456114

Unironic bottom signals. Everyone is trying to make fun of any bullish person. Like 2022.

62456190

62456215

item.title: >go into trades >be HVAC or plumber >Make 100k by year 3 Its that easy huh? — 22 replies — 29.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
>go into trades >be HVAC or plumber >Make 100k by year 3 Its that easy huh?

Open thread • Replies: 22 • Updated: 2026-07-07 17:23 UTC

OP

>go into trades
>be HVAC or plumber
>Make 100k by year 3

Its that easy huh?

Early replies (first 3)

62452524
>>62452507
No. Lol

62452597
>>62452507
This is the marketing tagline for trade schools. You'll note, like all marketing campaigns, there's alot they're not telling you.

62454749
>>62452507
nope

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62454913
>>62452524
>>62452597
>>62454749
why is that?

62454991
The real secret trade is aircraft maintenance. I get paid over $100K a year to sit around and wait for shit to break as a line technician. Reactive, not proactive maintenance. I bought a steam deck just to occupy my time when I’m not doing shit. Only downside is the night shift, but I’ve grown to enjoy it. I’m hoping I’ll get dayshift in a few years.

62455009
>>62454991
>in a few years
Bro has relegated himself to zombie status for years just for a measly 100k lmao. Night shift in your youth is fucking laughable

62455040
There's a oversupply in trades. People are having a hard time getting an apprenticeship. Why would not every illegal just get into plumbing if it's so easy and good money.

62455180
Lmao just work 60 hours a week year round goy.

62455189
>>62455040
You have to have a license to be a plumber, don't you? Illegals can't get licensed. Also the reason why Electrician and HVAC are still the /white man's trades/

62455203
>>62455189
When did you have a plumber last time. The world has changed my friend

62455249
>>62452507
It really is that easy. You can even start your own business within a few years of getting enough field experience and make even more money if that’s your thing. I would personally rather just not deal with that and be a wagie. I make enough money as it is and I don’t have to worry about taking the work home with me.

62455286
>>62455040
There is no oversupply. I will never have a hard time finding a job. hvacr work isn’t limited to just comfort cooling. There’s a demand for apprentices too because retention rate seems to be low, at least around where I’m at. Training apprentices takes time and the demand to fill in the roles of people retiring is there

62455334
>>62452507
I make more than that in year 2 and don't go through poop water and asbestos

62455349
>>62455189
Most of those plumbers are old white men now hiring the illegals. It's different state to state but here, as long as someone on site has a license(the old white man owner), everyone else is just a sub contractor not required to be licensed because the owners insurance covers them.

62455422
>>62454991
Did you go straight to getting your A&P, or did you join the military? I was thinking about joining the national guard to do rotarcraft maintenance. I would just go to A&P school, but I want to ride in them too. I figured crewchief experience would look good to wildland firefighting agencies or those transportation companies that do offshore shuttling.

62455429
>>62452507
>have BS & MS in mechanical engineering
>year 5 into HVAC engineering
>making 90k + incentive/performance bonus
>TC of $120-140k depending on year
>be the guy that signs the paycheck for trades people
Unless you own the company you aren't making over 100k in the trades.
That's a meme.
Most of the guys I pay are making at most $60k (medium CoL area), with tons of overtime they *might* make $75k. Which is still better than some non-STEM degree holder that has $100k in student loans.

"Go learn a trade" is the new "learn to code" and we all saw how that worked out. The moment people all flood into a singular field, that field dies for decades.

62455895
>>62455429
>Unless you own the company you aren't making over 100k in the trades.
>Most of the guys I pay are making at most $60k (medium CoL area), with tons of overtime they *might* make $75k
Our scale down here is $38 on the check (total benefits package ~$57) and we are the lowest in the state, probably also on the list of one of the lowest in the country even. You would only need to average around 47 hours a week to clear $100k. We have guys that willingly pull in crazy overtime and easily clear $100k. The other locals in the state make mid to upper 40s. Remember, that’s just the scale. That isn’t counting techs who make above that, which most techs do

62455955
>>62452507
>yeah my dad's boyfriend's wife's son's step dog's cousin makes $700k doing underwater HVAC brazing on the international space station only works 3 days every other leap year btfo collage fags

62455982
>>62452507
>>62454991
>tell your position and wage to the whole world so that everyone else will try doing the same and will end up flooding the market and lowering wages

62456079
>>62452507
Father started his HVAC business three years ago. Here in Prague, he went from $28k/year (when he was an employee) to around $250k/year (currently) and he keeps getting new customers and projects. He even got the official government inspector stamps for multiple HVAC fields, so he has nice passive income just from revisions.

62456093
>>62456079
I bet you guys are busier now because of the heat waves. Here in the US HVAC techs make the most money in the summer fixing and installing AC units

62456175
>>62456093
True, I think that is applicable globally. I don't work with him though, I have my own small IT company. But my father is now turning down many jobs and potential clients, and only works for the best paying customers, if even that. He always lived a frugal life and never much cared for wealth and that hasn't changed, so besides spending some money on his closest ones to improve their life, he simply has no need for more money, so every week he has situations like this
>phone rings, hotel owner on the line
>"Mr. Anonsfather, the wagies are being cooked, the guests are redeeming their bloody stays, please, come at once"
>"My apologies Mr. Shekelstein I simply don't have the time. The soonest I can get there is in three weeks"
>*click*
>sips on boomer lemonade, sitting in his garden, moisturized, in his lane, flourishing

So yeah, I think having your HVAC business might be nice

item.title: Months from now… years from now… people will wish they had recognized what was sitting right in front of them for years. — 8 replies — 22.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Months from now… years from now… people will wish they had recognized what was sitting right in front of them for years.

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-07-07 17:46 UTC

OP

Months from now… years from now… people will wish they had recognized what was sitting right in front of them for years.

Most people think they’ll sell XRP and simply buy back lower.

They still don’t understand what happens when real utility creates real demand, real economic activity, and real liquidity. That’s a completely different market.

As more organizations connect to the network, demand doesn’t just appear… it compounds. That’s how multi-trillion dollar markets are built.

Humanity has never witnessed a utility driven financial transformation on this scale. For the first time, we get to live through it together.

Excited for the next chapter.

Early replies (first 3)

62450170
It's had more than a decade for all that, ain't happening champ

62450645
>>62450165
Xrp was $3.20 in 2018 lol, shut up faggot

62450808
>>62450170
>>62450645
Obtuse gay jewish fingers typed these.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62451330
So bullish

62453970
>>62450808

62453976
>>62450165
>Months from now
How can you not see the wisdom of owning a coin that is down over 50% in 6 months.. everyone that is selling is a fool... BUY more... PLEASE PLEASE PLEASE buy more... I have to sell before the rent is due!!!

62454861
So fucking bullish XRP

62456256
>>62450165
KABOOOOOOOOOOOOM
>>>62456234

item.title: China Holds the Secret of Where the Oil Price Is Headed — 3 replies — 1.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
China Holds the Secret of Where the Oil Price Is Headed

Open thread • Replies: 3 • Updated: 2026-07-07 16:33 UTC

OP

>China's reduction of crude oil imports helped stop prices from reaching $200 a barrel, but it hasn't started buying again in the same amounts as before.
>If China buys oil again with gusto, prices will probably rise toward $80 a barrel, but if it doesn't, prices could stay lower, possibly in the $60-$65 range.
>China's future oil purchases could determine the oil price for years and have implications for inflation, interest rates, and the economy, with its current low imports potentially leading to billions of dollars less going to top OPEC+ producers.
https://archive.vn/JroFT

Early replies (first 3)

62454444
>>62454353
Haha sounds like the jew world order put all of its chips on china never becoming self-sufficient. Thats funny.

62454663
>>62454353
>>62454444
They just switched to Russia. And yes to other energies. I told the oil schizos the whole time. But they refused to sell high and are baggies now.

62455994
>>62454353
China's seaborne oil imports are going to drop even further in the long run. Most demand will switch to coal and electric alternatives, while domestic production and pipeline imports will cover most oil demand that remains. I would not be surprised if China imports a mere 2-3 million barrels per day by sea in 2035.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

item.title: /BBBY/ - Larry Cheng went onto Theppshow edition — 145 replies — 185.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
/BBBY/ - Larry Cheng went onto Theppshow edition

Open thread • Replies: 145 • Updated: 2026-07-07 17:21 UTC

OP

>rc buys 7,780,000 shares of BBBY, and $60-$80 strike calls worth 1,670,100 shares in March 2022

>rc sells shares and calls of BBBY in august 2022

>rc has a sitdown with a famous corporate raider, Carl Icahn in october 2022

>rc offers 400 million for BBBY in December 2022

>pitchbook (best M&A software suite in the world, costing 5 figures + per year) lists BBBY(now known as BBBYQ or DK-Butterfly-1) as sold via a leveraged buyout on January 13 2023

>a default on the ABL with JPM happened on the same day that pitchbook shows a leveraged buyout, the terms of that ABL specify that a default is triggered if a change of control happens, a change of control is defined as an acquisition of 40% or more of the voting power by any person or group.

>one month later, February 2023 RC tweets “someone tell the government to stop shooting down my balloons”, alluding to bad actors delaying the Bed Bath m&a

>the GameStop CEO then became a BBBYQ chapter 11 creditor and debtor

>Larry cheng (who helped RC build Chewy, and is a current boardmember at GameStop, and a close personal friend to RC) went onto theppshow podcast (which is dedicated to BBBY recovery theories) 8 months after BBBY filed for chapter 11, and 2 months after shares were cancelled

>OverStock - who is the most famous company to battle illegal naked shorts, who even released a crypto dividend 5 years ago - purchased the BBBY IP, and owns 55% of TZero - the only approved blockchain entity who can issue debt instruments and securities

>OverStock - whos now traded under ticker BBBY - has issued warrants with dates that match GME's warrants, and with a CUSIP-6 that matches BBBYQ's CUSIP-6, proving BBBYQ has already begun m&a activity with BBBY


REQUIRED READING: bbbwhy.com

>Larry Cheng and Bill Pulte have gone onto theppshow podcast

jake2b started a youtube channel to access quality BBBYQ easier than scrolling twitter posts:

https://www.youtube.com/@jake2b

previous thread:
>>62417410

Early replies (first 3)

62438274
>Larry Cheng was inside the PP show

62438733
Good morning my beautiful Towelkings.

62438734
uh... guys... you might want to check your accounts...

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62446238
>>62445569

62446242

https://www.timesofisrael.com/us-hockey-team-owner-suspended-after-social-media-spat-with-fan-over-gaza-war/

HOLY SHIT ITS FROM 2024. Imagine what him seething at RC has done to him since?

62446288
>>62446242
Hes 60 years old whats the joke here?

62446344
>>62446242
<suspended


Lmao so nothing happened to him except a meaningless press release BBBYbbqers cant stop losing

62446386
>>62446344
the real fun starts when the rico roundup is in full swing. But even better is when cash and new equity is distributed to previous BBBYQ holders.

Guess what happens when tzero gets involved.

62446441
>>62446386
>Guess what happens when tzero gets involved.

Your $0 stays $0

62447283
CUSIP-6

62447659
Ryan Cohen confirmed AGAIN that bathroom gods have won by posting on X that shows he has ties to the Icahn family. Shills will immediately come out of the woodwork now that I bring this up. It's over shills, we won. It's been 100% confirmed.

62447686
>>62447659
it's not brett icahn's fiance the birthmarks don't match

62447756
Baggie general

62447918
>>62447756

62448131
lamestop holders are seething because they missed out on the true moass. bathroom gods are the chosen ones. they trusted the plan above all. the cancellation of shares was the ultimate IQ test. weaker people couldn't handle that. now they are filled with regret.

62448146
>>62448131
The silo around BBBY was truly impressive. Superstonk made no mention of it. /GME/ was a full blown silo as well. It wasn't until I looked at the option chain on BBBY and saw the insane volatility that made me look in to it further. Greatest decision of my life.

62449186
>>62449050
all of them are short bbbyq. i guarantee it

62449344
>>62449050
>>62449186
If you are assassinated, does your short position get closed? Asking for Brian Thompson

62449448
>>62449344
Nope. His estate would get liquidated to pay off to buy back the shares.

62450370
CUSIP-6

62450461
>>62448146
The silo around eBay has become palpable

62452261
she has high res photos on her facebook. you can compare the birthmarks on her arm. the redditors are stupid as fuck it's demoralizing.

62452270
it seems that sultan didn't tweet about teddy like i thought he did. fuck's sake.

62452287
>>62452270
Its right here can you stop whining like a fatherless attention starved faggot? Youre scaring the hoes.

https://x.com/Almaadeed/status/2072817361862717939

62452325
>>62452287
ok good it displays the gamestop.com preview on the twitter page but the link he posted is to teddy.com which redirects to gamestop.com

62452826
>>62450461
The silo around BBBYQ has become Papaliable

62454181
CUSIP-6

62454621
>>62454181
baste

62455277
Pulte, Ryan, and Sultan are cooking up Teddy literally as we speak. You can't make this shit up.

62455555
Mister Groobie?

62456168
>>62455277

item.title: VOO and Chill — 192 replies — 145.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
VOO and Chill

Open thread • Replies: 192 • Updated: 2026-07-07 16:48 UTC

OP

The more I look into investing and compare the historical returns of certain ETFs / individual stocks it seems like the whole philosophy of “VOO and Chill” is equal parts really smart and retarded
It’s like a no brainer for money but even now as long as the market is in a growth period shouldn’t you at least do QQQ?
It seems so simple that’d you miss a lot of upside but the returns are honestly not bad and extremely consistent
How accurate is this assessment? It’s both so simple and guaranteed that everyone should it but also too safe so you end up missing out on more upside
I’m talking 5-20 years investing here not trading

Early replies (first 3)

62400515
Boomer funds are gay and lame

62400554
>>62400515
What qualifies as one
Why
What crypto do you want to shill

62400560
boglehead method is the tried and tested way, buy the market with an index fund that has a low fee, invest monthly, do it consistently for decades and you WILL be rich
but biz/anons don't care about being rich when they're older, they want to be rich NOW
greed is a deadly sin

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62431402
>>62431374
A small price to pay for a low expense ratio

62431920
>>62431131
Anyone trying to beat VOO over decades.

62431922
>>62431920
Feel like VUG has consistently beaten VOO though

62431971
>>62431922
Contrarian.
I own VUG and don't own VOO tho :)

62432023
>>62431920
fair enough
thoughts on a younger person doing QQQM?

62434054
Anyone have thoughts or realized returns and experiences on VOO + satellite
accounts instead of just VOO

62435372
Was gonna try to go into this DRAM dip but decided to just do more VOO
But idk a higher return ETF still sounds promising and like a no brainer whether it’s SOXX or QQQM but QQQ has so much crossover with VOO it’s just redundant and only good for growth seasons
I guess this is why VOO and Chill exists to just stop thinking

62436303
>>62431920
What about QQQ though

62436357
i boughted my daily VOO
i'll keep dealing the real world like a good goyim in the mean time.

62436478
>>62436357
huzzah brother

62436499
>>62400493
Where my VGT bros at?

62436507
>>62400636
>Yes and I'm sure that used car and maybe a holiday you can buy with it when you're 65 from cashing out all your investments will be great.....
You say this as if the average /biz/ shitcoin gambler is performing better than VOO which is definitely not the case.

62436510
>>62428031
Your IQ is so actually low. Holy shit.

62436538
>>62436510
Do you think DRAM has value, honest question, and for how long - if so, what dip should one wait for
>>62436499
VGT and QQQ are only doing better thanks to tech having such strong performances for years now, correct?
VOO seems safe since you don't know the future, but on the other hand, thinking the future of the market for the next decades wont always be tech focused seems so short sighted it's silly.....maybe I should start DCA into VGT?

62436562
>>62400493
>VOO
it's EVOO for me lads

62438775
Yep bros
Bought some more VOO and am waiting on my DRAM position to go green so I can sell and not have to deal with thematic ETFs no high expense ratios ever again
Simple as

62440169
>>62431374
>VOO is managed by 2 girl bosses
VOO is passively managed. These 2 girls are not like Kathy Wood picking stocks.

62442752
>>62431374
They could boil alive eat grannies alive for all I care as long as they get the money going, what kind of faggot ass train of thought is this. They could become billionaires and I would not give a shit as long as it keeps compounding. "The people making me rich are foids so it makes me icky" is unironically the foidest take I have ever read.
>>62430902
A year ago I started investing for the first time in my life (I'm in my mid 20s) and thought I was very smart and I would just outperform the market 1% profit at a time. After a whole year I only managed to get a net benefit of a 3% after wasting many hours and carrying heavy bags. I learnt my lesson. VOO and chill.

62442988
>>62442752
>They could become billionaires and I would not give a shit as long as it keeps compounding.

The portfolio managers of VOO have 0 control over whether VOO compounds or not. VOO is simply an ETF that tracks the S&P 500. Their only job is to make sure VOO follows the S&P 500. Once the fund is set up, there really is not much for them to do. I guess if they haven't automated removing and adding companies to the composition of VOO, then they do that.

62444521
Let’s all say thank you to Jack Bogle

62444531
>>62400515
and yet boomers hold 90% of the wealth and growing...

62445651
>>62421799
>5+ yr 60% drawdown event happens
>buy cheaper voo

62446571
>>62444521
thanks chad bogle

62447653
>>62445651
Kek it really is that simple

62450991
>>62446571
Thanks Chad Bogle!

62454214
>>62413718
Felix Prehn has never been an investment banker. He is an ex artist and wine salesman, now grifter on YouTube who makes money from his "academy" fees (up to 10k for shitty training). He's a regular scammer, like many in this segment.

62455460
It feels good to be a VOO’er

62455475
>>62400493
My retirement funds are $70k and in VOO, anything better to put all that in?

62455990
>>62455475
Look into VPMAX

62456048
>>62455990
Neat

item.title: I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job. — 96 replies — 94.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job.

Open thread • Replies: 96 • Updated: 2026-07-07 17:07 UTC

OP

I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job. I'm not young, 40 next year, and don't have a job history or references. High school graduate.
How fucked am I?

Early replies (first 3)

62449979
I live in California, too.

62449982
What did you do for the last 23 years? Hard to help without more context. Do you have skills?

62449989
>>62449982
Depression and NEET means not in education employment or training.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62451832
>>62451403
Wow shouldn't have cut internet mom lol

62451837
Healthcare or zogbot
Neither a comfy choice, but assuming healthcare, you can start sweeping floors then move up to some sort of auxiliary nurse, nursing assistant, then nurse eventually. You could reach 6 figs, but the path would take a fair amount of humiliation, wiping floors and boomer feces

62451874
>>62451745
This anon is offering something genuinely good for seemingly free.
I'm just noting that

62452257
>>62451063
OP check out this guy sno0ks on YouTube, he has just gone through a similar life experience to you and has vlogged about it: https://m.youtube.com/watch?v=VQsdy_yaQAQ&ra=m

62452288
Go to school
Get work experience
Get a real job
Jesus Christ most people figure this out when they’re 18 years old you god damn retard.

62452842
>>62451745
There's nothing to save, I have two teeth that are broken off at the gum line and the gums have grown over them, another tooth lost the back half above the gum line and my lower right molar is broken on top and has a chunk missing.
They all have to come out.

62452847
>>62452288
What school? I'm already a high school graduate.
>>62451821
Food stamps, dum dum.

62453356
What about trying to start a business? Self employment? Why do my only options have to be depressing bullshit?

62453476
>>62453356

62453498
>>62453356
Both are better (best?) options for wealth if you don't plan on going to college and also marrying someone who went to school. Both preferably masters degrees or higher.

62453510
>>62449961
I have an online friend who was a loser NEET for like 10 years, he's still a loser but he started editing videos for youtube channels for a % of revenue and one of them popped off and he's made around 220k in 2 years. If you are autistic about something and use the internet, it's possible, though you have to get lucky and work hard still

62453537
>>62453356
You need some capital to get started, whatever biz you want to open. And since you sound incredibly lazy and your body most likely haven't a single gram of muscular mass, you probably wont do anything outside, correct?
Then you need to make cash from your computer.

I started scalping and flipping TCGs back in 2016 with $4K invested. Slow start but it eventually paid off after a bunch of smart picks on my end (no sealed, i focused on singles at release i thought could become staples, extremely risky and you need to be very good at the game itself to correctly identify unnoticed bangers). Once i secured more cash i could start to focus on vintage staples and that's where the $$ are. Cashed out 100K total so it turned out pretty nicely.

If you have zero you can try to make AI pornslop, twitch content or stuff like that. But starting from scratches, with zero visibility, zero social skill and no determination in a field ultra-saturated with mega grinders who want to succeed... i don't think you'll go very far. Not to discourage you, but what you have showed us of your personality so far tells me you most likely will end up as a beggar living under a bridge.

62453557
>>62453510
Video editing is a shitload of work hours, if you reads OP shitpost you'll quickly understand it's out of his reach. He's basically asking if we can give him a winning lottery ticket, and when we tell him it doesn't work that way, he whines.

62453686
>>62453557
You would need a computer for that and an Internet connection. The desktop unused to have died several years ago and I'm only allowed to use the wifi with my phone.
I would have to move out completely first.

62453688
Also, if you guys could calm down on the personal attacks? I didn't do anything to deserve that shit. Whatever boggeyman lives in your head isn't me.

62453723
God, it feels like I'm having panic attack after panic attack.

62453813
>>62453723
honest to god you should go work on a boat/ship, get paid to roam the seas and hear stuff on the radio and sleep in the middle of the darkness of the ocean and occasionally see whales.

62453981
>>62449961
This is why I tell my neet friend to grt out and go grab a job, even packing groceries before you reach 40 something. YOU WILL REGRET NEETING

62454510
seconding personal home aide/caregiver
gotta get your shoe in the door somehow

https://www.uscareerinstitute.edu/online-caregiver-school
tuition: $485
2-4 months online part time training

62454560
Become a trans prostitute

62455526
I guess I need to see a dentist soon. That's next to do, I guess

62455565
Oh, is this the bump limit? Threads about to slide off the board?
>>62454560
You're insane or a glowie.

62455632
>>62454560
Can always start with trans feet pics. Get them all pedicured and female-passing first of course.

62455644
Are you artistically inclined?
No shit, you may be able to draw furry porn. Those guys still pay out the ass for commissions and even though AI is a thing a lot of furries refuse to use it to get their porn.

62455814
>>62449961
Good call making this on a Monday so you get the extra seethe from wagies that had to go back to work.

62455832
>>62455814
I hadn't thought of that. Lol.

62455843
>>62450003
You're practically dead already, I mean come on, can you really say you've lived at all as a NEET? And Hegseth in his infinite retardation raised the enlistment cut-off to 42 so it's an option.

62456054
>>62455832
You're not completely screwed, by the way. You'll have to work entry-level jobs for a bit to build references and show stability, though. In order of priority highest to lowest:
>phone/internet
You're posting here so I assume you have either one of these. Phone is important.
>dental
Try federally funded health centers, medicaid, dental schools, county clinics, and free clinics
>getting care for your mom
You can't even take care of yourself so try to find your mom some care. Try eldercare locator to start.
>transportation
If you're rural and don't have transportation that needs to change. The end goal here should be getting your license to drive, but if you never drove before that could take months so you'll need to find something in the meantime.
>job
If you're rural this might have to wait until you have transportation, but the goal is entry-level work to build a resume/show stability. If they ask about work history tell them you were out of the workforce for family caregiving and health reasons.

also apply for any assistance programs you can. have no shame in doing so. they're there for people in your situation

62456107
>>62449961
Your options are disability, construction or the military. Say you were caring for your mom. I'd try the military first while you are still eligible. If they declare you ineligible then either try trade school or use it as an excuse to claim disability.

62456122
I can't get an angle on my teeth to take photos, I'll have to get a dental mirror later.

item.title: BTC Price Down. What's Your Plan? — 117 replies — 54.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
BTC Price Down. What's Your Plan?

Open thread • Replies: 117 • Updated: 2026-07-07 15:58 UTC

OP

BTC is down again, but are you buying the dip or waiting for lower prices?

>Do you think this is just a normal correction, or is there more downside ahead? What's your strategy?

Early replies (first 3)

62426518
I've been shorting this garbage all month

62426527
>>62426518
Wow, you've got good profit by now then?

62426535
>>62426527
up 80% on some 2x short BTC etf

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62442563
>>62426512
I am DCAing in

62442650
>>62442503
memecoins pump faster. I made money on Ansem and some Solana gems

62442655
>>62439758
never trust centralised services

62442658
buy btc goys

62442668
>>62442658
oy vey

62443383
>>62442650
Well, that's good for you.
>>62442655
Same. Self-custody first, then I check if the protocol has had multiple audits.

62443384
>>62442658
Do it man!

62443930
>>62442668
Goy

62444179
I'm waiting for October, but if it goes below 55k I'll probably start slurping. Everyone who says crypto I'd dead is retarded. Not ripping = dead is their logic.

62444587
>>62426537
That's never going to happen, max will be around 45K. My plan is to DCA, I got in around 58K, stake a few on babylon, I'm waiting to see if there will be more dump

62444608
>>62444587
You really trust the Babylon protocol? It's my first time looking into it and I see I could earn 0.02+ BTC every month. $1200+ every month sounds much better than sitting in my Ledger.

62444998
>>62444608
really

62445005
>>62426512
I'm selling at 100k. I've had enough of holding and should have sold last year. I wanna get out of this shit, it's too much stress.

62445018
>>62427357
do not pull

62445689
>>62444998
DYOR

62446816
>>62445018
Who's plebs rn?

62447133
>>62445005
regrets?

62447140
Ngmi

62447522
>>62442255
Just keep going

62448616
>>62426537
this is probably correct : (

trump and the political class already made their money off crypto last couple years

62448797
>>62427359
Surely the bottom would be zero, no?

62449904
>>62448797
people like to imagine when theyre on drugs

62450375
Why

62450895
Buy Bitcoin

62450912
>>62426512
Buy mote bitcoin and wait for next halving to dump on the normies.

62453141
>>62444608
The idea is to make BTC work even while holding just like ETH instead of leaving it idle, and I like the idea of self custody on that protocol

62454511
>>62426638
this but unironically

62454976
I sold at 115k so no more plans for me.

62455432
>>62454976
Ok

62455827
>>62426535
How you doing now, bobo?

62455741

62455743

62455746

62455830

62455892

62455920

62455929

62456083

item.title: AI is dying — 31 replies — 17.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
AI is dying

Open thread • Replies: 31 • Updated: 2026-07-07 16:22 UTC

OP

>Over the weekend, I got a message from a tech company CEO outlining his TOTAL BAN ON AI across his organization. There are no exclusions. It is not limited to generative AI. It is not temporary. It is a full stop.
>It turns out that the corporate dream of a single “100x” human managing hordes of AI agents isn’t the optimal way to run a business

Sorry not sorry, you've been warned multiple times but kept throwing money into casino chips aka AI stocks.

https://www.inc.com/joe-procopio/the-first-company-wide-ai-ban-just-hit-my-inbox-heres-what-it-means/91357535

Early replies (first 3)

62449971
>The Ban on AI Research
>The CEO’s company had been dealing with a slump in conversion rates for the better part of 2026
>Turns out, AI-assisted research had resulted in casting a much wider net for prospects and dragged in many that were not nearly a fit. In some cases, the companies were defunct, and in one case, the company didn’t exist at all. The best the CEO could figure is that AI had just inferred it into existence

62449974
>The Ban on AI Agents
>One of the agents broke, in that was unable to scrape data it needed to generate a report. Its solution was to just generate the report anyway and then to freak out about the sudden change in what that report was saying, now that it was missing foundational information but treating it as if that entire business line had suddenly gone to zero.
>“It took a week to figure it out, because the guy who created the agent wasn’t the guy responsible for the report, and he was stalling while re-running months’ worth of reports by hand.”

62449977
>The Ban on Vibe Coding
>The reporting failure was the trigger for the ban.
>“When I discovered we had two different versions of the same module in our platform, connected to two different databases filled with two different sets of data stored in two completely different and unrelated [schemas], the next day I put the ban in place.”

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62449981
>>62449963
I'm happy for you, or sorry that happened to you.

62449983
>>62449963
>believing corporate media slop intended to make poorfags stay out

62450012
>>62449983
>they don't want us to make money

62450055
>>62449963
>single “100x” human managing hordes of AI agents isn’t the optimal way to run a business

It is, but there is so little "100x" human and definitely no one is working for that specific company.

62450135
>>62450012
Speaking for myself, I do not like the idea of poors entering my country club.

62450162
>>62449963
It isn't dying, but people are waking up to the fact that it's not "Jarvis, run my company" situation and that there is a hard skill curve to coordinating agent pipelines.

62450194
>>62450012
True, but the game is already rigged for the casino either way.

62450337
Not reading all that but AI isn't going anywhere, the pace may slow down but it's not going to be binned off.

62450351
CEOniggers once again proven to be 40 IQ retards.

62450414
>>62449963
Our CEO is going all-in on AI for software development and it has boosted our throughput by 2-3x. It's not slop code, either.
We have unlimited tokens.
t. SWE at startup

62450423
>>62449963
Over the weekend my ceo told me we are adopting more ai

62452074
>>62450414
>It's not slop code
lol. LMAO even

62452087
>inc.com
What
>a tech ceo
Which company

62452090
>>62450012
>rich people investing 3 trillion into AI the last 4 years
>Nvidia a 5 trillion dollar company
>the AI market is over now, because it's over
Makes you think.

62452160
>>62450135
Exactly. There is the door so would you kindly leave?

62452209
Kek my company banned it years ago when some high level executive fed sensitive financial data to chat gpt. It qas back when chat gpt was new and novel and he was probably a boomer that didn't know what chatbots are and thought it was a super intelligence.

62452880
AI is a technology and it won't go way, it will evolve into different tools that will enhance the production. The problem is that corporations are using as an excuse to control the resources and make big data centers to get more info from you and sell it.

62452896
>>62449963
This article is a month old, fucktard.

62452909
>>62452896
Precisely, baggie.

62452925
>>62452909
continuation pattern

62452935
>>62452925
lmao

62452942
>>62452935
>you: heh my pattern is bearish
>"i disagree, I think it's bullish"
>REEEEEEEE TA SUCKS FUCK YOU

62453140
>>62452942
I never implied that. I implied you're delusional if you believe exponential growth is any sustainable long-term.

62453190
>>62453140
Probably because you are retarded.

62454009
>>62452074
All the code is written and reviewed by hand. We have custom AI agents for repetitive tasks and custom tool calls error-checking.
Works great, amazing ROI

62454042
>>62449963

There is no way I am going to ever write a regular expression or a complex SQL statement without AI EVER AGAIN.
AI solution saves time and is easy to verify.

62455423
>>62449963
its joever

62455925
>>62454009
We don’t even review our AI code anymore. Our CTO admitted the pace at which we are vibe coding quality code and feature delivery far outweighs the cons and our SWEs can keep up with helping us troubleshoot but can’t keep up with actually reviewing the code. We launched a new product and between me and one other person built the whole thing over 2,000 PRs from scratch in 3 weeks. Currently customers on it generating 6 figures in new revenue monthly, almost zero issues and when they do happen 98% of the time I just take the error throw it at Claude and it’s fixed 10m later. Crazy. I have zero coding experience btw, though I was a product manager for many years so led multiple teams of SWEs and have a clear advantage over most non coders in using AI for that and many other reasons. I keep thinking I should sell my house because I realize I’m pretty early to understanding how truly powerful this shit is and I think a lot of people are going to lose their jobs within the next few years, though I hope I’m wrong.
